creator: imura@af.airnet.ne.jp
text:
On Mon, Jan 29, 2001 at 08:26:53PM +0900, Akinori MUSHA wrote:
> > - Update www/linux-lesstif-ns to latest version(0.92.17).
> > - Use bzip2 archive instead of gzip archive.
> > - Do linux(8) before configure, because if linux mode
> >   is not active, this port won't be configurable/buildable
> >   and will show weird error messages (kind of these
> >   messages will confuse some people).
> > - Please remove "patch-aa", in addition to the following
> >   patch.
> 
> Good work!  However, lesstif-0.92.17.tar.bz2 seems to have gone and I
> couldn't fetch it.  Judging from the broken link lesstif-test.tar.bz2
> -> lesstif-0.92.17.tar.bz2 left in the ftp directory, it might have
> been a transient test version.

You've judged quite correctly.

> Would you investigate the case and submit a new update?

From Release Policy (http://www.lesstif.org/release-policy.html),

  the "pico" version number (the third number) also carries a meaning :
  only even numbers are used for releases, the odd numbers are the
  development versions between releases.

According to the cvs log, latest even number is "6".
(http://cvs.sourceforge.net/cgi-bin/cvsweb.cgi/lesstif/test/configure.in?cvsroot=lesstif)

So, my update should have been
- Update www/linux-lesstif-ns to the latest "stable" version(0.92.6).

Here is the new distinfo
------------
MD5 (lesstif-0.92.6.tar.bz2) = def7876f248228eb0f0441ba8ef931ad
MD5 (lesstif-0.92.6-i18n.patch) = 27b7c5a42f12634b581c9b8d8efac881
------------

Regards,

- R. Imura
